raw_consumer_statement: My Citi card offers a flex pay plan where you can take certain purchases and move them into the flex plan. I made a purchase online from XXXX XXXX and moved it into the flex plan immediately, thinking I was going to keep the purchase. I found a better deal and canceled the order. I verified via phone with both XXXX XXXX and Citi that the item was credited back to my card and that Citi would reverse the flex plan. I pay my bills every month without question. Huge mistake because I noticed after the fact that I was still paying on the flex plan that was supposed to have been canceled. We spoke to Citi and had them rise the inquiry up to management level. They are telling us that we actually weren't charged and the we weren't paying on the flex plan at all. We have our statements and payment records with the bank that show we did pay monthly on the flex plan that supposedly was credited when the return happened. Citi insists that these charges never occurred. My payments and statements show otherwise. I don't know what they are looking at on their end but they should at least have access to our payment and statement info. This is extremely frustrating. We, like millions of other people struggle month to month. When we buy anything of significance it is a thought out decision. We shouldn't have to pay for something we never bought. I am contemplating legal means but thought it prudent to start here.
